passing_score_scale: Percentage (80% passing for all CDL knowledge tests)

testing_centers.notes: Knowledge tests administered at state DMV or driver licensing offices. No Pearson VUE or Prometric involvement. Skills test administered at state-approved CDL third-party testing sites or DMV.

id_requirements: Valid state-issued identification required. Must have a valid non-CDL driver's license to apply for a CDL learner's permit. DOT medical certificate (DOT physical) required for CDL issuance.
exam_rules: CDL knowledge tests administered on computer at state DMV offices. No personal reference materials permitted during testing. HazMat endorsement additionally requires TSA security threat assessment (STA) — background check fee and processing time apply separately. CDL learner's permit required before scheduling the skills (road) test.
renewal_options: CDL valid for 4–8 years depending on state. Renewal requires current DOT medical certificate. HazMat endorsement must be renewed every 5 years (TSA STA also renewed). Contact your state DMV for state-specific renewal requirements.

retake_policy.notes: Typical 14-day waiting period between failed attempts; exact policy varies by state. Some states impose additional fees for each retake attempt.

official_study_resources.title: CDL Commercial Driver's License Manual
official_study_resources.notes: Free CDL manual available from FMCSA and individual state DMV websites. States may publish their own version of the manual with state-specific content.
